node.statement: (Application of automatic purity to B(H)). For B(H): the smallest positive face dimension is 3 (from rank-2 states), so ℓ = 3 and G(B(H)) ⊇ {1, 2}. The automatic purity theorem gives: under m ≤ 2 constraints, all extreme points are pure. This is the content of Weis-Shirokov Corollary 7 for the quantum case. For abelian C(X): ℓ = 1, G = ∅, so even 1 constraint can produce non-pure extreme points (e.g., convex combinations of point masses satisfying a moment constraint).
